title: You vs You
html:
Yahir lays out the you vs you frame: life is a single-player wrld where the only true competition is with yourself, and outcomes derive from your own actions. Continual self-sharpening, control of what's controllable, and unconditional self-belief are the load-bearing beams.

TL;DR

  • you vs you is framed as a solo-player wrld — outcomes depend on you, and the iron-blade metaphor casts self-sharpening as continual maintenance.
  • Genuine self-belief in front of the mirror is the precondition; without it the you vs you frame collapses.
  • The Stoic move — control only what's within your control — is folded in as a derivative of you vs you.
  • do a good present surfaces as the operative ethic: coexisting with other single-player wrlds while keeping your own progression accelerated.
  • Re-mentioned in 2024-09-25 entry of same name; this is the original 2024 articulation.

you vs you · do a good present · wrld philosophy · self belief · stoicism
